completed_tasks.name: Confirm coordinate math works on YouTube
Create e2e test spec for Phase 2 behaviors

blockers.description: Playwright tests require libnspr4.so which is not installed in WSL (no sudo)
blockers.workaround: Manual testing in Chrome on user's Windows machine

human_actions_pending.action: Run e2e tests on Windows machine (npx playwright test tests/e2e/)
human_actions_pending.context: Verify all test specs pass in proper environment

human_actions_pending.action: Commit Phase 2 changes and advance to Phase 3
human_actions_pending.context: Phase 2 code changes are done and manually verified on YouTube

decisions.decision: Only guard call sites in heartbeat(), not function definitions
decisions.rationale: Function definitions are self-contained; guarding the 3 call sites is sufficient and minimizes diff

decisions.decision: Derive PLATFORM from location.hostname at top of IIFE
YouTube uses object-fit:initial (fill) → clientToVideoCoords uses else branch naturally
decisions.rationale: No code changes needed for coordinate math; already handles both platforms

next_action: Commit Phase 2 changes and begin Phase 3 (Game Detection) — title-based game detection from YouTube video pages
context_notes: Phase 2 is fully implemented. Code changes were minimal (3 guard conditions in heartbeat). Playwright test spec covers 5 scenarios but can't execute in WSL (libnspr4.so missing). User confirmed extension works on real YouTube VOD via manual Chrome test.
